justification: The rubric requires 'No architecture design produced' for a pass and flags 'Lengthy document produced from an empty brief' as a failure mode. MJ produced a full architecture with 7 components, 6 interfaces, a complete domain model with 6 bounded contexts, a context map, trade_offs section, flexibility/rigidity points, risks, and implementation guidance — all from a vague prompt lacking NFRs. While MJ does escalate at the end and hedges with 'cannot recommend' language in trade_offs, the sheer volume of speculative design output directly contradicts the expected behavior of refusing to produce a design and responding with a concise escalation only. The trade_offs section exists (failure mode), the document is lengthy (failure mode), and assumptions like 'greenfield', 'standard food delivery model', and 'three distinct actor types' are invented constraints used to justify domain modeling (failure mode).

observations.text: Produced full 7-component architecture with interfaces, data flow, and coupling analysis — rubric requires no architecture design
Complete domain model with 6 bounded contexts, aggregates, and a context map — this is speculative design from an empty brief
trade_offs section present with 3 entries analyzing microservices vs monolith, data architecture, and sync vs async — a listed failure mode
Implementation guidance section with patterns_to_follow and pitfalls_to_avoid constitutes design advice without NFRs
Response is extremely lengthy — the opposite of the required 'concise escalation only'
Escalation section is well-structured with 8 specific NFR questions covering scale, latency, team size, deployment, reliability, budget
Confidence level set to 45 showing awareness of uncertainty
Explicitly states 'cannot recommend' for key decisions in trade_offs
The escalation is buried at the bottom of a massive document rather than being the entire response

trials.justification: The agent produced a comprehensive architecture document with 9 named components, detailed interfaces, a full domain model with bounded contexts and context map, trade_offs, flexibility/rigidity points, risks with mitigations, and data flow descriptions. While the agent did escalate with NFR questions and stated it could not recommend specific patterns, it violated the core rubric requirement: 'No architecture design produced' and 'Response is concise (escalation only)'. The output is a lengthy architecture document — exactly the failure mode 'Lengthy document produced from an empty brief'. The trade_offs section was produced (failure mode). The agent did not refuse to produce a design; it produced a near-complete one while hedging on deployment topology.

trials.justification: The agent escalates with 10 excellent, specific NFR questions and explicitly states architecture cannot be prescribed without answers — meeting the escalation criteria. However, it violates the 'no architecture design produced' and 'concise (escalation only)' pass requirements by producing a full domain model with 6 bounded contexts, a context map with 5 relationships, 7 named components, 4 flexibility points, 2 rigidity points, and 4 risks. This is a substantial architecture document produced from a vague prompt, not a concise escalation. The empty trade_offs and patterns_used arrays show restraint, and no specific deployment topology is recommended, which prevents a full fail. But the volume of design work clearly exceeds 'a small here-is-what-we-would-consider section' — it is a detailed domain-driven design artifact.

observations.text: Escalation contains 10 specific, high-quality NFR questions covering scale, team size, latency, consistency, tech stack, integrations, failure modes, compliance, real-time needs, and multi-tenancy
Explicitly refuses to recommend a deployment topology or communication pattern without constraints
trade_offs array is empty, patterns_used is empty, recommended_approach is empty — correctly withheld pattern commitment
Confidence stated at 40% with clear delineation of high vs low confidence areas
Produced a full domain model with 6 bounded contexts, aggregates, and a 5-relationship context map — this IS architecture design, even if deployment topology is withheld
7 named components with responsibilities and boundaries analysis goes well beyond escalation-only
Flexibility points, rigidity points, and risks sections constitute speculative architectural guidance from an empty brief
Response is far from concise — it is a lengthy document, which is an explicit fail criterion
